behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior across directory depth. MOCK-MISS: this is not just parsing logic; it is watcher->path discovery handoff. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: recursive/subdirectory event delivery is OS and watcher-implementation dependent, and in-process fakes can incorrectly always deliver events. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires real watch coverage of "subdirectory of the watched root," which is exactly where fake emitters can mask missed recursive events.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp root directory, create a nested subdirectory, write a new file there, and assert one transformed output appears in the output directory without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Minimum failure boundary is module interaction: request intake + dedup window state + pipeline trigger suppression. MOCK-MISS: can fail at handoff/state propagation across modules, not only in one pure function. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no AI output dependency; no multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected Unit because behavior text says "second processing request" within a "configurable time window," implying orchestration/state across components rather than isolated function-only logic. Rejected System because wording does not require real filesystem events.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake service, dedup store/cache, and pipeline dispatcher in-process; submit the same path twice within the configured window and assert only one dispatch occurs, then outside the window assert dispatch occurs again.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS event multiplicity. REAL-INFRA: must observe real watcher/event semantics from the operating system. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watchers typically emit a chosen pattern and can miss duplicate/burst patterns that trigger real double-processing bugs. Rejection gate: rejected Integration due to explicit phrase "operating system emits multiple filesystem events."
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory, perform a write pattern known to emit multiple events (e.g., create+append+rename or editor-style atomic save), and assert exactly one processing/output record for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is runtime interaction with real filesystem lifecycle events and watcher re-subscription. REAL-INFRA: directory delete/recreate notifications and watch invalidation are OS-level behaviors. MOCK-HIDE: fake watchers may preserve subscriptions unrealistically and hide rebind bugs. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires "removed and then recreated while the daemon is running" and recovery "without a restart," which depends on real watch handle behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon against a real directory, remove the directory, recreate it at the same path, then place a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering correctness depends on real file metadata and event timing under concurrent arrivals. MOCK-MISS: this is ordering/state handoff, not a single local function. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: filesystem timestamp resolution, creation-time availability, and event race behavior are OS/filesystem-specific; in-process fakes can impose idealized ordering. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text ties ordering to real "creation timestamps" for files that "arrive at the same time," which is where real FS semantics matter.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real watched directory, create several files nearly simultaneously with controlled creation times (or controlled sequence where timestamps differ), then assert processing/output order matches filesystem creation timestamps.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between real write-in-progress behavior and watcher-triggered processing. REAL-INFRA: needs real file I/O timing/flush behavior during active writes. MOCK-HIDE: fakes usually deliver fully written content or deterministic completion, masking partial-read/early-pickup bugs. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly states "still being written to when the arrival event fires," which requires real I/O timing conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory; write a large file in chunks with delays to keep it in-progress after first event; verify daemon does not process until final chunk/close, then processes exactly once with complete content.
